=== おじょか古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |名称 = おじょか古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = 志島11号墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島512-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|18|39.68|N|136|53|20.03|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline|name=志島11号墳（おじょか古墳）}} |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 埴製枕ほか副葬品多数・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 三重県指定史跡「おじょか古墳」 |有形文化財 = 埴製枕（三重県指定文化財） |特記事項 = }} '''おじょか古墳'''（おじょかこふん、王女丘古墳{{Sfn|北村|1985|p=134}}/志島11号墳）は、三重県志摩市阿児町志島にある古墳。三重県指定史跡に指定され、出土埴製枕は三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。[[昭和]]30年代に石室が開けられて副葬品が取り出されたほか、[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 墳丘周辺はこれまでの宅地開発で削平を受けており、墳形は明らかでない{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。墳丘表面では[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]が検出されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、西南西方向に開口する{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。石室全長6メートル・最大幅2メートルを測る{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。緑色千枚岩の板石積みによって構築され、羨道部は「ハ」字形に広がるとともに、玄室は開口部から奥壁に広がる羽子板形を呈する{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。こうした石室の特徴は北部九州地方の石室（類例は[[関行丸古墳]]（[[佐賀県]]）『志摩のあけぼの展 -考古学からみた志摩の歴史-』志摩市教育委員会、2010年、pp. 1319。）に類似する点で注目される{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。また玄室内には朱が塗られている。 石室内からの出土品としては、装身具類（方格T字鏡・珠文鏡・玉類・櫛）・鉄製品類（斧・鎌・刀子・直刀・剣・槍・鉾・鏃・鋲留短甲）・漆塗網代状木製品（胡簶の一部か）がある{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。また天井石直上の墳丘盛土内からは鉄刀が出土したほか、調査前の出土品として埴製枕・鉄刀類がある{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。これらのうち特に埴製枕は、被葬者の頭を乗せる土製の枕で、高さ約28.5センチメートル・幅約32.6センチメートルを測る{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。円筒形脚台部（円筒埴輪形状）の上に頭を乗せる座部を乗せ、その背後に直弧文・鰭飾りを施した衝立部を付し、表面には赤色顔料が塗られる{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。古墳出土の埴製枕は、本古墳のほか[[燈籠山古墳]]・[[五条猫塚古墳]]（[[奈良県]]）・[[産土山古墳]]（[[京都府]]）で知られるのみで貴重であるとともに、本古墳例はそれらと比較して装飾豊かな点で注目される{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}（または5世紀後半{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}）頃と推定される{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 {{Coord|34|18|39.68|N|136|53|20.03|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=title|name=おじょか古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしよかこふん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: 全体加筆修正、画像追加と更新。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = おじょか古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 石室開口部 |別名 = 志島11号墳 |所属 = [[志島古墳群]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島512-1 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 39.80 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 埴製枕・武器・短甲・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 三重県指定史跡「おじょか古墳」 |有形文化財 = 埴製枕（三重県指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Mie |アイコン = |ラベル = おじょか
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''おじょか古墳'''（おじょかこふん、王女丘古墳{{Sfn|北村|1985|p=134}}/志島11号墳）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある古墳。[[志島古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。三重県指定史跡に指定され、埴製枕は三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 三重県東部、[[志摩半島]]先端部の安乗崎・大王崎の間、緩やかに東に突出する海食台地の尾根最高所（標高約25メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）に発掘され、[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 墳丘周囲は宅地開発で削平されており、元の墳形は明らかでない{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。墳丘外表では[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]が検出されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、西南西方向に開口する{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。羽子板形玄室・ハ字形前庭部・石障を特徴とする初期的形態の横穴式石室であり、北部九州の石室に類似するとして注目される。石室内からは、埴製枕や玉類のほか銅鏡・鉄刀・鉄剣・鉄槍・鉄鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・鉄鎌・刀子など多数の金属製品が出土しており、それら副葬品の様相からは近畿中央部・九州地方・朝鮮半島との多様な交流が示唆される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}（または5世紀後半{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}、ON46（TK208古段階）-TK208型式期{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}）頃と推定される。一帯は[[紀伊半島]]から[[伊勢湾]]西岸・三河以東に進むうえでの交通上要衝であり、広域の海上交通を掌握した被葬者像が想定される{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。志摩地域では最古級の古墳として突如現れる様相を示すが、その後は一度途絶え、[[6世紀]]末から再び[[志島古墳群]]・畔名古墳群が築造される。北方には[[志摩国分寺]]が所在し、志摩国府の所在も推定されることから、のちの[[志摩国]]の成立への端緒として評価しうる点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 古墳域は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に三重県指定史跡に指定され、埴製枕は[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * 戦時中、墳頂部に防空用見張台・半鐘台の設置{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。 * [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）頃から、周囲の宅地造成で石室外面の露出開始{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1960年]]（昭和35年）、[[伊勢湾台風]]で石室露出{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）、石室奥壁の一部取り外し。埴製枕・直刀・鉄鏃などの出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1967年]]（昭和42年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土（阿児町教育委員会、[[1968年]]に概報刊行）{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）3月28日、三重県指定史跡に指定。 * 石室実測調査（[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[1992年]]に報告）。 * [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）3月13日、埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2012年]]度（平成22-24年度）、出土金属製品の保存処理（志摩市教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Ojoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、西南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 石室全長：6メートル * 玄室：長さ2.9メートル、幅2.15メートル（奥壁）・1.7メートル（玄門）、高さ1.4メートル * 前庭部（羨道）：幅1.1メートル（玄門）・1.95メートル（入り口） 石室の石材は緑色千枚岩で、板石積みによって構築される。玄室の平面形は奥に向かって広がる台形（羽子板形）で、前庭部（羨道）は入り口に向かってハ字形に開く。玄室の壁際には板石を石障として置く。また玄室の床面には小円礫を敷き、その下の粘質土上面では朱が検出されている。前庭部（羨道）に天井石はない{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石室の構造は九州地方との関係が指摘されており、これまでに[[釜塚古墳]]（福岡県）、[[ヤンボシ塚古墳]]（熊本県）、[[目達原古墳群|目達原大塚古墳]]・[[関行丸古墳]]（佐賀県）などとの類似が指摘される。 Ojoka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Ojoka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Ojoka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|前庭部 Ojoka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|埴製枕（三重県指定文化財）}}
{{small|[[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕 (裏面).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|埴製枕 背面}}]] 石室内からは、1961年（昭和36年）の発掘の際に埴製枕・直刀・鉄鏃などが、1967年（昭和42年）の発掘調査の際に多数の副葬品が出土している。1967年（昭和42年）調査で出土した金属製品は次の通り{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ !colspan=2| !出土位置 !1968年報告 !1992年報告 !2016年報告{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}} |- |rowspan=2|'''鏡''' |'''方格T字鏡'''||玄室奥壁中央||1||1||1 |- |'''珠文鏡'''||玄室入口北側||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''半球形飾金具'''||玄室奥壁南側||3||3||3 |- |rowspan=4 colspan=2|'''刀''' |玄室奥壁中央||3||rowspan=4|13
個人蔵2||rowspan=4|14 |- |玄室側壁南側||3 |- |羨道中央||2 |- |天井石直上||4 |- |rowspan=4 colspan=2|'''剣''' |玄室奥壁中央||1||rowspan=4|2||rowspan=4|3 |- |羨道中央||1 |- |羨道奥||1 |- |天井石直上||1 |- |colspan=2|'''槍'''||天井石直上||2||2||2 |- |colspan=2|'''鉾'''||玄室入口北側||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''鏃'''||玄室側壁南側||多数||34
個人蔵2||56 |- |colspan=2|'''短甲'''||天井石直上||1||0
個人蔵7片||9片 |- |rowspan=2 colspan=2|'''斧'''||玄室入口南側||2||rowspan=2|5||rowspan=2|5 |- |玄室入口北側||3 |- |colspan=2|'''鎌'''||玄室奥壁北側||2||2||2 |- |colspan=2|'''刀子'''||天井石直上||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''不明鉄製品'''|| || ||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''鉤状鉄製品'''|| || ||2||5 |} 埴製枕は、おじょか古墳で最も特徴的な出土遺物になる。被葬者の頭を乗せる土製の枕で、高さ約28.5センチメートル・幅約32.6センチメートルを測る。円筒形脚台部（円筒埴輪形状）の上に頭を乗せる座部を乗せ、その背後に直弧文・鰭飾りを施した衝立部を付し、表面に赤色顔料を塗る。古墳出土の埴製枕は、おじょか古墳のほか[[燈籠山古墳]]・[[五条猫塚古墳]]（[[奈良県]]）・[[産土山古墳]]（[[京都府]]）で知られるのみであるが、いずれも形態は異なり、おじょか古墳例はそれらと比較しても装飾性が豊かである{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。 方格T字鏡は、方格規矩鏡のT・L・Vの文様のうちL・Vの文様が欠落したもので、九州地方に分布の中心があり、伝蓮ヶ池横穴群（宮崎県）の吾作銘帯方格T字八鳳鏡と同一意匠の可能性が高いとされる{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。鉾は錫装鉾で、類例は[[マロ塚古墳]]（熊本県）でのみ知られるが形態は大きく異なっており、大加耶・百済系の銀装鉾と類似する{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 おじょか古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|装身具類 おじょか古墳出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品類 == 文化財 == === 三重県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 埴製枕（考古資料） - 志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1995年（平成7年）3月13日指定[http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕]（三重県教育委員会）。。 * 史跡 ** おじょか古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）3月28日指定[http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]（三重県教育委員会）。[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間） - おじょか古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県史|volume=資料編 考古1|publisher=三重県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告 -金属製品編-]|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告4|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf 志摩市の文化財]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志摩市の文化財|2018}}}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/ojyokakohun.pdf おじょか古墳発掘50年記念シンポジウム「おじょか古墳と5世紀の倭」記録集]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=下村登良男|year=1989|chapter=おじょか古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=伊藤保|title=王女丘古墳とその出土品について|date=1966|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=34|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=志摩・おじょか古墳発掘調査概要 -三重県志摩郡阿児町大字志島-|publisher=阿児町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=志摩国の古墳|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]考古学研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=豊田祥三|title=伊勢における円筒埴輪の生産と展開|date=2001|journal=研究紀要|publisher=三重県埋蔵文化財センター|volume=|number=10|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[志島古墳群]] * [[向山1号墳 (若狭町)]]、[[千足古墳]] - おじょか古墳と同時期の九州系横穴式石室を有する古墳。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]、[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕] - 三重県教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:おしよかこふん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = おじょか古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 石室開口部 |別名 = 志島11号墳 |所属 = [[志島古墳群]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島512-1 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 39.80 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 埴製枕・武器・短甲・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 三重県指定史跡「おじょか古墳」 |有形文化財 = 埴製枕（三重県指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Mie |アイコン = |ラベル = おじょか
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''おじょか古墳'''（おじょかこふん、王女丘古墳{{Sfn|北村|1985|p=134}}/志島11号墳）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある古墳。[[志島古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。三重県指定史跡に指定され、埴製枕は三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 三重県東部、[[志摩半島]]先端部の安乗崎・大王崎の間、緩やかに東に突出する海食台地の尾根最高所（標高約25メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）に発掘され、[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 墳丘周囲は宅地開発で削平されており、元の墳形は明らかでない{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。墳丘外表では[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]が検出されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、西南西方向に開口する{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。羽子板形玄室・ハ字形前庭部・石障を特徴とする初期的形態の横穴式石室であり、北部九州の石室に類似するとして注目される。石室内からは、埴製枕や玉類のほか銅鏡・鉄刀・鉄剣・鉄槍・鉄鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・鉄鎌・刀子など多数の金属製品が出土しており、それら副葬品の様相からは近畿中央部・九州地方・朝鮮半島との多様な交流が示唆される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}（または5世紀後半{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}、ON46（TK208古段階）-TK208型式期{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}）頃と推定される。一帯は[[紀伊半島]]から[[伊勢湾]]西岸・三河以東に進むうえでの交通上要衝であり、広域の海上交通を掌握した被葬者像が想定される{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。志摩地域では最古級の古墳として突如現れる様相を示すが、その後は一度途絶え、[[6世紀]]末から再び[[志島古墳群]]・畔名古墳群が築造される。北方には[[志摩国分寺]]が所在し、志摩国府の所在も推定されることから、のちの[[志摩国]]の成立への端緒として評価しうる点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 古墳域は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に三重県指定史跡に指定され、埴製枕は[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * 戦時中、墳頂部に防空用見張台・半鐘台の設置{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。 * [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）頃から、周囲の宅地造成で石室外面の露出開始{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1960年]]（昭和35年）、[[伊勢湾台風]]で石室露出{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）、石室奥壁の一部取り外し。埴製枕・直刀・鉄鏃などの出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1967年]]（昭和42年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土（阿児町教育委員会、[[1968年]]に概報刊行）{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）3月28日、三重県指定史跡に指定。 * 石室実測調査（[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[1992年]]に報告）。 * [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）3月13日、埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2012年]]度（平成22-24年度）、出土金属製品の保存処理（志摩市教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Ojoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、西南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 石室全長：6メートル * 玄室：長さ2.9メートル、幅2.15メートル（奥壁）・1.7メートル（玄門）、高さ1.4メートル * 前庭部（羨道）：幅1.1メートル（玄門）・1.95メートル（入り口） 石室の石材は緑色千枚岩で、板石積みによって構築される。玄室の平面形は奥に向かって広がる台形（羽子板形）で、前庭部（羨道）は入り口に向かってハ字形に開く。玄室の壁際には板石を石障として置く。また玄室の床面には小円礫を敷き、その下の粘質土上面では朱が検出されている。前庭部（羨道）に天井石はない{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石室の構造には九州地方との関係が認められており、これまでに[[釜塚古墳]]（福岡県）、[[ヤンボシ塚古墳]]（熊本県）、[[目達原古墳群|目達原大塚古墳]]・[[関行丸古墳]]（佐賀県）などとの類似が指摘される。 Ojoka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Ojoka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Ojoka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|前庭部 Ojoka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|埴製枕（三重県指定文化財）}}
{{small|[[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕 (裏面).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|埴製枕 背面}}]] 石室内からは、1961年（昭和36年）の発掘の際に埴製枕・直刀・鉄鏃などが、1967年（昭和42年）の発掘調査の際に多数の副葬品が出土している。1967年（昭和42年）調査で出土した金属製品は次の通り{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ !colspan=2| !出土位置 !1968年報告 !1992年報告 !2016年報告{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}} |- |rowspan=2|'''鏡''' |'''方格T字鏡'''||玄室奥壁中央||1||1||1 |- |'''珠文鏡'''||玄室入口北側||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''半球形飾金具'''||玄室奥壁南側||3||3||3 |- |rowspan=4 colspan=2|'''刀''' |玄室奥壁中央||3||rowspan=4|13
個人蔵2||rowspan=4|14 |- |玄室側壁南側||3 |- |羨道中央||2 |- |天井石直上||4 |- |rowspan=4 colspan=2|'''剣''' |玄室奥壁中央||1||rowspan=4|2||rowspan=4|3 |- |羨道中央||1 |- |羨道奥||1 |- |天井石直上||1 |- |colspan=2|'''槍'''||天井石直上||2||2||2 |- |colspan=2|'''鉾'''||玄室入口北側||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''鏃'''||玄室側壁南側||多数||34
個人蔵2||56 |- |colspan=2|'''短甲'''||天井石直上||1||0
個人蔵7片||9片 |- |rowspan=2 colspan=2|'''斧'''||玄室入口南側||2||rowspan=2|5||rowspan=2|5 |- |玄室入口北側||3 |- |colspan=2|'''鎌'''||玄室奥壁北側||2||2||2 |- |colspan=2|'''刀子'''||天井石直上||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''不明鉄製品'''|| || ||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''鉤状鉄製品'''|| || ||2||5 |} 埴製枕は、おじょか古墳で最も特徴的な出土遺物になる。被葬者の頭を乗せる土製の枕で、高さ約28.5センチメートル・幅約32.6センチメートルを測る。円筒形脚台部（円筒埴輪形状）の上に頭を乗せる座部を乗せ、その背後に直弧文・鰭飾りを施した衝立部を付し、表面に赤色顔料を塗る。古墳出土の埴製枕は、おじょか古墳のほか[[燈籠山古墳]]・[[五条猫塚古墳]]（[[奈良県]]）・[[産土山古墳]]（[[京都府]]）で知られるのみであるが、いずれも形態は異なり、おじょか古墳例はそれらと比較しても装飾性が豊かである{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。 方格T字鏡は、方格規矩鏡のT・L・Vの文様のうちL・Vの文様が欠落したもので、九州地方に分布の中心があり、伝蓮ヶ池横穴群（宮崎県）の吾作銘帯方格T字八鳳鏡と同一意匠の可能性が高いとされる{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。鉾は錫装鉾で、類例は[[マロ塚古墳]]（熊本県）でのみ知られるが形態は大きく異なっており、大加耶・百済系の銀装鉾と類似する{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 おじょか古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|装身具類 おじょか古墳出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品類 == 文化財 == === 三重県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 埴製枕（考古資料） - 志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1995年（平成7年）3月13日指定[http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕]（三重県教育委員会）。。 * 史跡 ** おじょか古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）3月28日指定[http://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]（三重県教育委員会）。[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間） - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=下村登良男|year=1989|chapter=おじょか古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=伊藤保|title=王女丘古墳とその出土品について|date=1966|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=34|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=志摩・おじょか古墳発掘調査概要 -三重県志摩郡阿児町大字志島-|publisher=阿児町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=志摩国の古墳|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]考古学研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=豊田祥三|title=伊勢における円筒埴輪の生産と展開|date=2001|journal=研究紀要|publisher=三重県埋蔵文化財センター|volume=|number=10|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[志島古墳群]] * [[向山1号墳 (若狭町)]]、[[千足古墳]] - おじょか古墳と同時期の九州系横穴式石室を有する古墳。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]、[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕] - 三重県教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:おしよかこふん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 http:// -> https:// (www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = おじょか古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 石室開口部 |別名 = 志島11号墳 |所属 = [[志島古墳群]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島512-1 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 39.80 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 埴製枕・武器・短甲・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 三重県指定史跡「おじょか古墳」 |有形文化財 = 埴製枕（三重県指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Mie |アイコン = |ラベル = おじょか
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''おじょか古墳'''（おじょかこふん、王女丘古墳{{Sfn|北村|1985|p=134}}/志島11号墳）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある古墳。[[志島古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。三重県指定史跡に指定され、埴製枕は三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 三重県東部、[[志摩半島]]先端部の安乗崎・大王崎の間、緩やかに東に突出する海食台地の尾根最高所（標高約25メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）に発掘され、[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 墳丘周囲は宅地開発で削平されており、元の墳形は明らかでない{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。墳丘外表では[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]が検出されている{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、西南西方向に開口する{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。羽子板形玄室・ハ字形前庭部・石障を特徴とする初期的形態の横穴式石室であり、北部九州の石室に類似するとして注目される。石室内からは、埴製枕や玉類のほか銅鏡・鉄刀・鉄剣・鉄槍・鉄鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・鉄斧・鉄鎌・刀子など多数の金属製品が出土しており、それら副葬品の様相からは近畿中央部・九州地方・朝鮮半島との多様な交流が示唆される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}（または5世紀後半{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}、ON46（TK208古段階）-TK208型式期{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}）頃と推定される。一帯は[[紀伊半島]]から[[伊勢湾]]西岸・三河以東に進むうえでの交通上要衝であり、広域の海上交通を掌握した被葬者像が想定される{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。志摩地域では最古級の古墳として突如現れる様相を示すが、その後は一度途絶え、[[6世紀]]末から再び[[志島古墳群]]・畔名古墳群が築造される。北方には[[志摩国分寺]]が所在し、志摩国府の所在も推定されることから、のちの[[志摩国]]の成立への端緒として評価しうる点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 古墳域は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に三重県指定史跡に指定され、埴製枕は[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * 戦時中、墳頂部に防空用見張台・半鐘台の設置{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。 * [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）頃から、周囲の宅地造成で石室外面の露出開始{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1960年]]（昭和35年）、[[伊勢湾台風]]で石室露出{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）、石室奥壁の一部取り外し。埴製枕・直刀・鉄鏃などの出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * [[1967年]]（昭和42年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土（阿児町教育委員会、[[1968年]]に概報刊行）{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）3月28日、三重県指定史跡に指定。 * 石室実測調査（[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[1992年]]に報告）。 * [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）3月13日、埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2012年]]度（平成22-24年度）、出土金属製品の保存処理（志摩市教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Ojoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、西南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 石室全長：6メートル * 玄室：長さ2.9メートル、幅2.15メートル（奥壁）・1.7メートル（玄門）、高さ1.4メートル * 前庭部（羨道）：幅1.1メートル（玄門）・1.95メートル（入り口） 石室の石材は緑色千枚岩で、板石積みによって構築される。玄室の平面形は奥に向かって広がる台形（羽子板形）で、前庭部（羨道）は入り口に向かってハ字形に開く。玄室の壁際には板石を石障として置く。また玄室の床面には小円礫を敷き、その下の粘質土上面では朱が検出されている。前庭部（羨道）に天井石はない{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石室の構造には九州地方との関係が認められており、これまでに[[釜塚古墳]]（福岡県）、[[ヤンボシ塚古墳]]（熊本県）、[[目達原古墳群|目達原大塚古墳]]・[[関行丸古墳]]（佐賀県）などとの類似が指摘される。 Ojoka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Ojoka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Ojoka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|前庭部 Ojoka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|埴製枕（三重県指定文化財）}}
{{small|[[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕 (裏面).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|埴製枕 背面}}]] 石室内からは、1961年（昭和36年）の発掘の際に埴製枕・直刀・鉄鏃などが、1967年（昭和42年）の発掘調査の際に多数の副葬品が出土している。1967年（昭和42年）調査で出土した金属製品は次の通り{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+ !colspan=2| !出土位置 !1968年報告 !1992年報告 !2016年報告{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}} |- |rowspan=2|'''鏡''' |'''方格T字鏡'''||玄室奥壁中央||1||1||1 |- |'''珠文鏡'''||玄室入口北側||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''半球形飾金具'''||玄室奥壁南側||3||3||3 |- |rowspan=4 colspan=2|'''刀''' |玄室奥壁中央||3||rowspan=4|13
個人蔵2||rowspan=4|14 |- |玄室側壁南側||3 |- |羨道中央||2 |- |天井石直上||4 |- |rowspan=4 colspan=2|'''剣''' |玄室奥壁中央||1||rowspan=4|2||rowspan=4|3 |- |羨道中央||1 |- |羨道奥||1 |- |天井石直上||1 |- |colspan=2|'''槍'''||天井石直上||2||2||2 |- |colspan=2|'''鉾'''||玄室入口北側||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''鏃'''||玄室側壁南側||多数||34
個人蔵2||56 |- |colspan=2|'''短甲'''||天井石直上||1||0
個人蔵7片||9片 |- |rowspan=2 colspan=2|'''斧'''||玄室入口南側||2||rowspan=2|5||rowspan=2|5 |- |玄室入口北側||3 |- |colspan=2|'''鎌'''||玄室奥壁北側||2||2||2 |- |colspan=2|'''刀子'''||天井石直上||1||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''不明鉄製品'''|| || ||1||1 |- |colspan=2|'''鉤状鉄製品'''|| || ||2||5 |} 埴製枕は、おじょか古墳で最も特徴的な出土遺物になる。被葬者の頭を乗せる土製の枕で、高さ約28.5センチメートル・幅約32.6センチメートルを測る。円筒形脚台部（円筒埴輪形状）の上に頭を乗せる座部を乗せ、その背後に直弧文・鰭飾りを施した衝立部を付し、表面に赤色顔料を塗る。古墳出土の埴製枕は、おじょか古墳のほか[[燈籠山古墳]]・[[五条猫塚古墳]]（[[奈良県]]）・[[産土山古墳]]（[[京都府]]）で知られるのみであるが、いずれも形態は異なり、おじょか古墳例はそれらと比較しても装飾性が豊かである{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。 方格T字鏡は、方格規矩鏡のT・L・Vの文様のうちL・Vの文様が欠落したもので、九州地方に分布の中心があり、伝蓮ヶ池横穴群（宮崎県）の吾作銘帯方格T字八鳳鏡と同一意匠の可能性が高いとされる{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。鉾は錫装鉾で、類例は[[マロ塚古墳]]（熊本県）でのみ知られるが形態は大きく異なっており、大加耶・百済系の銀装鉾と類似する{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 おじょか古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|装身具類 おじょか古墳出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品類 == 文化財 == === 三重県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 埴製枕（考古資料） - 志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1995年（平成7年）3月13日指定[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕]（三重県教育委員会）。。 * 史跡 ** おじょか古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）3月28日指定[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]（三重県教育委員会）。[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間） - おじょか古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県史|volume=資料編 考古1|publisher=三重県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告 -金属製品編-]|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告4|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf 志摩市の文化財]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志摩市の文化財|2018}}}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/ojyokakohun.pdf おじょか古墳発掘50年記念シンポジウム「おじょか古墳と5世紀の倭」記録集]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=下村登良男|year=1989|chapter=おじょか古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=伊藤保|title=王女丘古墳とその出土品について|date=1966|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=34|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=志摩・おじょか古墳発掘調査概要 -三重県志摩郡阿児町大字志島-|publisher=阿児町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=志摩国の古墳|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]考古学研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=豊田祥三|title=伊勢における円筒埴輪の生産と展開|date=2001|journal=研究紀要|publisher=三重県埋蔵文化財センター|volume=|number=10|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[志島古墳群]] * [[向山1号墳 (若狭町)]]、[[千足古墳]] - おじょか古墳と同時期の九州系横穴式石室を有する古墳。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]、[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕] - 三重県教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:おしよかこふん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:三重県指定史跡]]

{{Japanese burial mound |Name = Ojoka Kofun |Image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}} 512-1 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |Shape = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Daiosaki|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property * Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]]

The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property * Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:おじょか古墳]]

Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Daiosaki|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property * Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:おじょか古墳]]

Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property * Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]おじょか古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Daiosaki|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property * Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site * Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - designated in 1995. * Historic Site ** Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[ja: [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]]

Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja: [[ja:おじょか古墳]]

Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ojoka Burial Mound]][[Category:dd]] [[ja: [[ja:おじょか古墳]]

Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Ago-cho Shima, Shima City, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県志摩市阿児町志島}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamazuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Burial mounds in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ojoka Burial Mound]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja: [[ja:おじょか古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database
