Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at aqqroximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at aqqroximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at aqqroximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at a11roximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ojo Kofun]][[Category:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ojoka Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ojoka Kofun}}

Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |Artifacts = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = |Label = Ojoka
Kofun |Label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |Alternate name = Shima No. 11 Kofun |Group = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |Size = |Burial facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |map = Japan Mie |Icon = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database
