Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original shape of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-typed main chamber, a V-typed forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |map = Japan Mie |Icon = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |Special notes = |map = Japan Mie |Icon = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |Icon = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture Cultural Property Database Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation, with its clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. Artifacts discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow designated a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == Artifacts == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar artifacts from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designated in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays artifacts from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal Artifacts-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = Mie Prefecture Designated other_designation (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_designation, with its clay pillow designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the formation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been other_designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_other_designation, with its clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_other_designation = Mie Prefecture Designated other_other_designation (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label position = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been other_designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_other_designation, with its clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = Mie Prefecture Designated other_designation (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been other_designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_designation, with its clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been other_designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_designation, with its clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |Era = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = Mie Prefecture Designated other_designation (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been other_designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_designation, with its clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural Designations == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible Cultural Property ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = Mie Prefecture Designated other_designation (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been other_designation1 as a Mie Prefecture other_designation, with its clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture other_designation. * 1995: Clay pillow other_designation1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture other_designation === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; other_designation1 in 1995. * other_designation ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}} 512-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}. Part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎}} and {{ill|Cape Daiō|ja|大王崎}}, the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at [[Category:pp error]]approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as {{ill|Kamatsuka Kofun|ja|釜塚古墳}} and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}
