The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} 512-1|coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]]

Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} 512-1|coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿児町安乗}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} 512-1|coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿児町安乗}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}}

The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} 512-1|coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿児町安乗}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}}

Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿児町安乗}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}}

The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ojoka Kofun]] [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q127163732}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q127163732]] Wikidata name: Ojoka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] 512-1|coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿児町安乗|1=Agochō-Anori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657267}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q127163732}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ojoka Kofun |image = [[File:Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|280px]] |caption = Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Shima No. 11 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] 512-1|coordinates = {{Coord|34|18|39.80|N|136|53|20.00|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ojoka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Unknown |area = |burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Clay pillow, weapons, short armor, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |Satellite = |built = Mid-5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Ojoka Kofun) |Tangible cultural property = Clay pillow (Mie Prefecture Designated Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Mie |type = |map_dot_label = Ojoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Ojoka Kofun''' (おじょかこふん, also 王女丘古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|lt=Ago-cho Shima, Shima City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. Part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it has been Historic Site1 as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site, with its clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the Shima Peninsula, between {{ill|Anorisaki|lt=Cape Anori|ja|安乗崎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anorisaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿児町安乗|1=Agochō-Anori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657267}} and [[:en:Cape Daiō|Cape Daiō]], the mound occupies the highest point of a marine terrace ridge at approximately 25 meters above sea level. Excavated in 1961 and investigated further in 1967, the original type of the mound has been obscured due to residential development. Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been found on the surface, while the burial facility comprises a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west-southwest. The chamber features a paddle-shaped main chamber, a V-shaped forecourt, and barrier stones, resembling early horizontal stone chambers from Kyushu. burial goods discovered include a clay pillow, beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron spears, iron halberds, iron arrowheads, short armor, and various other iron tools, suggesting cultural exchanges with the Kansai region, Kyushu, and the Korean Peninsula. The mound is dated to the mid-5th century, during the Kofun period, and is believed to represent a pivotal figure who controlled maritime routes across the Kii Peninsula, Ise Bay, and further eastward. The mound, along with later tombs in the Shima and Azuna clusters, signifies the region's importance in the typeation of the Shima Province. == Archaeological History == * Wartime: Observation and alarm bells were installed at the mound's peak. * 1957: Residential development exposed the outer stonework. * 1960: Stone chamber exposed during Typhoon Isewan. * 1961: Excavation uncovered a clay pillow, swords, and arrowheads. * 1967: Further investigation revealed additional grave goods. * 1969: Designated as a Mie Prefecture Historic Site. * 1995: Clay pillow Historic Site1 a Mie Prefecture Tangible designation1. == burial == The stone chamber measures as follows: * Total length: 6 meters * Main chamber: 2.9 m (length), 2.15 m (width at the back), 1.7 m (width at the entrance), 1.4 m (height) * Forecourt: 1.1 m (entrance width), 1.95 m (outer width) The chamber walls are constructed of green slate slabs, and the floor is covered with small pebbles over clay treated with red pigment. The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q127163732}}

The design suggests connections to similar tombs in Kyushu, such as [[:en:Kamatsuka Kofun|Kamatsuka Kofun]] and others in Kumamoto and Saga Prefectures. == burial goods == The chamber yielded significant finds, including: * Bronze mirrors * Clay pillow: Notable for its intricate decoration and reddish pigment, it stands out among similar burial goods from other mounds. == Cultural designation1s == === Mie Prefecture Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ''' Clay pillow - Stored at the Shima Historical Folklore Museum; Historic Site1 in 1995. * Historic Site ''' Ojoka Kofun - Designated in 1969. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}} (Isobe-cho Hazama, Shima City) - Displays burial goods from Ojoka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Mie Prefecture History (Archaeology 1)|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie Prefecture History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460 |title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun (Shima No. 11 Kofun) -Metal burial goods-|publisher=Shima City Education Board|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|2016}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ojoka Kofun}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7 Ojoka Kofun] - Mie Prefecture designation1 Database {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|おじょか古墳|version=102697573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:おじょか古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] {{Commons category|Ojoka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q127163732}}
